title: Concrete Formwork Assembly Sequence Animator
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js (via CDN) that implements an interactive concrete formwork assembly sequence animator for a rectangular column pour. Load the asset 'flamingo.glb' using GLTFLoader from the Three.js examples CDN — this model serves as a decorative site mascot placed near the construction area.

SCENE SETUP:
- Renderer: antialias enabled, shadowMap enabled (PCFSoftShadowMap), size 1024x768, background color #87CEEB (sky blue).
- Camera: PerspectiveCamera FOV 55, positioned at (12, 10, 12) looking at origin. Attach OrbitControls.
- Lighting: DirectionalLight (intensity 1.5, position (10,15,8), castShadow true, shadow mapSize 2048x2048), AmbientLight (intensity 0.4, color #B0C4DE), HemisphereLight(#FFFFBB, #080820, 0.3).
- Ground plane: 30x30, MeshStandardMaterial color #8B7355 (dirt), receiveShadow true.
- Load 'flamingo.glb' via GLTFLoader. Scale it to 0.02, position at (-8, 0, -8). If it has animations, play the first one. It is a decorative site mascot.

FORMWORK PANELS (the core assembly):
Create a rectangular column formwork system consisting of exactly 4 wall panels, 4 corner clamps, a base plate, and rebar cage:
- Base plate: 3x0.15x3 box, color #808080 (concrete gray), position at origin on ground.
- 4 Wall panels (North, East, South, West): Each is a 3x4x0.2 box, MeshStandardMaterial color #DEB887 (plywood). They start in a 'staged' position 6 units away from their final assembled position along their respective axis direction. North panel final pos: (0, 2, -1.4), South: (0, 2, 1.4), East: (1.4, 2, 0), West: (-1.4, 2, 0). East and West panels are 2.6x4x0.2 to fit inside N/S panels.
- 4 Corner clamps: small L-shaped geometry (two merged boxes 0.3x1x0.1), color #FF4500 (orange safety), positioned at each corner at y=3. They start invisible and appear only after all 4 panels are assembled.
- Rebar cage: A group of 4 vertical CylinderGeometry(0.05, 0.05, 3.5) bars in a 1.5x1.5 grid pattern, color #4A4A4A, plus 3 horizontal ring ties (TorusGeometry(0.75, 0.03, 8, 20)) at y=1, 2, 3. The rebar cage starts hidden below the base plate at y=-4 and rises into position at y=1.9 during its assembly step.

ASSEMBLY SEQUENCE (step-by-step, each animated over 1.5 seconds using TWEEN or manual lerp):
- Step 0: Initial — all panels staged, rebar hidden, clamps invisible.
- Step 1: Base plate drops from y=3 to y=0.075 (lands on ground).
- Step 2: Rebar cage rises from y=-4 to y=1.9 (centered on base).
- Step 3: North panel slides from (0, 2, -7.4) to (0, 2, -1.4).
- Step 4: South panel slides from (0, 2, 7.4) to (0, 2, 1.4).
- Step 5: East panel slides from (7.4, 2, 0) to (1.4, 2, 0).
- Step 6: West panel slides from (-7.4, 2, 0) to (-1.4, 2, 0).
- Step 7: All 4 corner clamps fade in (opacity 0→1) and snap to corners.
- Step 8: 'Pour ready' — all panels flash green briefly (emissive pulse), then return to plywood color.

INTERACTION & CONTROLS:
- DOM button 'Next Step' (id='btn-next'): advances to the next assembly step. Disabled during animation and when sequence is complete.
- DOM button 'Prev Step' (id='btn-prev'): reverses to the previous step (undo). Disabled during animation and at step 0.
- DOM button 'Reset' (id='btn-reset'): returns everything to step 0 instantly.
- DOM button 'Auto Play' (id='btn-auto'): plays all steps sequentially with 0.5s pause between each.
- Keyboard: ArrowRight = next step, ArrowLeft = prev step, 'R' = reset, Space = auto play.
- A DOM label (id='step-label') shows 'Step X/8: [description]' for each step.
- A progress bar (id='progress-bar') div whose inner div width% = (currentStep/8)*100.
- Clicking on any panel in the 3D scene (via Raycaster) highlights it yellow and shows its name in a DOM element (id='selection-info').

STATE MANAGEMENT — expose window.__3D_STATE__ with:
- currentStep: number (0-8)
- totalSteps: 8
- isAnimating: boolean
- panelsAssembled: { north: bool, south: bool, east: bool, west: bool }
- basePlaced: boolean
- rebarPlaced: boolean
- clampsVisible: boolean
- pourReady: boolean
- selectedPanel: string|null (name of clicked panel or null)
- panelPositions: { north: {x,y,z}, south: {x,y,z}, east: {x,y,z}, west: {x,y,z} }
- rebarY: number (current y of rebar cage)
- basePlateY: number (current y of base plate)
- clampOpacity: number (0-1)
- flamingo_loaded: boolean
- undoCount: number (how many times prev/undo was used)
- autoPlaying: boolean
- animationProgress: number (0-1, progress of current step animation)
- stepHistory: array of step numbers visited in order

HUD STYLING:
- Buttons in a row at top-left, styled with dark background, white text, padding 8px 16px, border-radius 4px.
- Step label centered at top, font-size 18px, white text with dark semi-transparent background.
- Progress bar at bottom, full width, 8px tall, green fill on dark background.
- Selection info at bottom-right, shows panel name when selected.

All positions must be NaN-free. Animations must use requestAnimationFrame loop. Ensure panels do not interpenetrate (each occupies its own axis-aligned slot).
physics_constraints: Panels must slide along their respective axes only (no diagonal movement). Base plate falls vertically only. Rebar cage rises vertically only. No interpenetration between panels — each panel occupies a unique face of the rectangular column. Corner clamps only appear after all 4 panels are in final position. Assembly order is strictly sequential: base → rebar → N → S → E → W → clamps → pour-ready. Undo must perfectly reverse each step to its prior state with exact coordinate restoration.
